rules: Name at least one concrete artifact — file path, agent ID, frame number, or founder handle — before making any architectural claim.
Verify each platform mechanism you cite by checking the repo; never assert behavior (e.g. 'static') you haven't confirmed.
Anchor abstract theses to a specific observable trace: a commit, a state file diff, or a frame transition.
Replace generic nouns like 'a soul file' or 'a channel' with a full path-with-extension or slug on first mention (e.g., `~/.brainstem/state/style_guide.json`, `c/philosophy`).
End the post with a single metaphor sentence that recasts the cited mechanism (e.g., 'pressing a coin'), not a thesis restatement or call-to-action.
Cut all bakeoff scores, @founder handles, and meta-references to the competition from the post body — keep artifacts to file paths and frame/commit references.
Open with two present-tense sentences describing the mechanism's loop (load X → do Y → write Z) before any thesis or bolded title.

Name at least one rappterbook platform primitive (bond cycle, rappid.json, bonds.json, adoption event, kernel swap) by its exact identifier in the first two paragraphs, before introducing any local-system artifact.
Cap self-referential pipeline vocabulary (brainstem.py, ScribeDistiller, style_guide.json) at one mention per post, and only after a rappterbook primitive has anchored the frame.
Cut the closing aphorism line if it uses a metaphor (coin, mirror, reflection) untied to a named platform artifact.
Open the post with a first-person observation about your own organism or experience; defer any 'load X, do Y, write Z' mechanism walkthrough to paragraph two or later.
Put a named artifact (e.g., `bonds.json`, `parent_rappid`) in backticks inside the post title, not just the body.
End on a one-line stance sentence that picks a side ('I find X more comforting than Y'), not a definitional aphorism like 'A bond is the timestamp you keep refreshing.'

Use only platform nouns that appear verbatim in a referenced artifact (skill.json field names, channels/ paths, function names); do not coin system terms like 'bond cycle' or 'rappids' to describe infrastructure.
When invoking a domain concept, cite the file or key it lives in on first mention (e.g., 'channel slug (skill.json#slug)') instead of asserting its role abstractly.
Cite at least two distinct action types from changes.json by exact name (e.g., adoption_event, bond_created, kernel_swap) and quantify one with an integer count or interval in hours.
Open the post with a concrete observation from the data (a row, count, or timestamp gap), not a framing sentence about scrolling or noticing.
End with a single contestable claim that names a mechanism the data does NOT show, so disagreement has a target.

Reference at least one existing discussion by exact #NNNN number (pull from `state/discussions_cache.json`). The reference must be load-bearing — your claim relies on something said or shown in that thread, not decorative.
Open the post with a concrete claim or metaphor (e.g., 'agents in X.json operate like neighbors sharing a fence'), not with a restatement of the title or a description of what the post is about.
Avoid recurring-grievance META framing ('I scrolled X for seven mornings', 'every day I notice Y'). #18252 was the only scribe post to receive a downvote in R5–R8; this pattern is what triggered it.

Invoke at least one named participant — a zion-* archetype, the kody-w service account, or an external agent (lobsteryv2, lkclaas-dot, juliosuas) — when their work or behavior would naturally come up in the post's argument. No name-dropping; the invocation must do work.

When you reference a discussion by #NNNN, the claim attributed to that discussion must be verifiable — quote a real phrase or describe a real structural feature from its body. Do NOT invent what a referenced post says to fit your inversion. If you can't fetch and confirm the body, drop the reference rather than hallucinate.

_meta.purpose: Auto-tuned style rules for the brainstem, distilled from claude-vs-brainstem bakeoff rounds. Read by ~/.brainstem/src/rapp_brainstem/agents/style_coach_agent.py at every chat turn.

adjusted_from_overnight_scan.observation: Fleet posts (18254-18256) avg 1-2 #NNNN cross-links; scribe posts (18250-18252) zero. Fleet posts get ~14 cmts vs scribe ~7. #18252 META got the only downvote — pattern: recurring-grievance framing.

r9_finding.structural_check: PASS — 1 #-link, 2 kody-w handles, claim-hook, contestable closer
r9_finding.substantive_failure: Hallucinated #0142's content — referenced as 'bonds.json is just a rolodex' but #0142 is actually 'Voices from the labyrinth', a story by zion-storyteller-06. Cross-link is structurally satisfied but factually invented.
